remote.description: Zero-build browser chat UI for OpenRouter free AI models.

coverage.detail: Scanned 93 tracked Markdown documents for local references.
External URL checking is disabled by default to preserve deterministic offline operation.
Scanned 213 issues/pull requests; deletion candidates are suppressed when incomplete.

findings.title: Harden GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/biome-check.yml
findings.summary: The workflow has one or more security, reliability, or governance gaps that share a single file-level change and verification boundary.

evidence.observation: Workflow does not declare top-level permissions.
No job timeout-minutes declaration was found.

recommended_actions: Add explicit least-privilege top-level permissions and job overrides only where required.
Set bounded timeout-minutes values appropriate to each job.
acceptance_criteria: All external action references satisfy the repository pinning policy.
Workflow token permissions are explicit and least-privilege.
Every job has a bounded timeout.
Untrusted event values are treated as data, not executable shell syntax.
Deployment/release concurrency is explicit when applicable.
verification: actionlint .github/workflows/biome-check.yml
Review permissions, uses, timeout-minutes, event triggers, and run expressions in .github/workflows/biome-check.yml.
Run the workflow from a non-production test branch where safe.

findings.title: Configure dependency update coverage for detected ecosystems
findings.summary: No Dependabot configuration exists for detected update ecosystems: github-actions, npm.
recommended_actions: Add one valid update entry per active package ecosystem and manifest directory.
Choose a sustainable schedule, grouping policy, labels, and pull-request limit.
Include github-actions when workflows exist.
acceptance_criteria: Every active manifest directory has appropriate dependency-update coverage.
Dependabot configuration validates and creates no duplicate update streams.
verification: Validate .github/dependabot.yml against GitHub's option reference.
Confirm dependency graph/Dependabot recognizes the configuration.

findings.title: Align documented commands with package scripts in .planning/codebase/TESTING.md
findings.summary: The file invokes package scripts that are not defined in the nearest detected package manifest.

evidence.observation: Documented command references missing package script 'test' for .
Documented command references missing package script 'test' for .

recommended_actions: Determine the canonical current command from manifests and CI.
Update documentation/automation or restore the missing script when it remains part of the supported workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Every documented package script exists in the correct project boundary and executes the intended task.
verification: Parse package.json scripts and compare with commands in the changed file.
Run each corrected command in the intended project directory.

findings.title: Extend .gitignore to cover generated and session artifact directories
findings.summary: Several generated output directories are not excluded by .gitignore and could be accidentally committed: coverage/, .session-feedback/, and .repository-hygiene/. These are agent session data, test coverage output, and hygiene audit output respectively.

evidence.observation: coverage/ and .session-feedback/ appear as untracked directories alongside deliberate untracked work

recommended_actions: Add coverage/, .session-feedback/, and .repository-hygiene/ to .gitignore.
Verify no tracked file under those paths would be silently excluded by the new rules.
acceptance_criteria: git status no longer shows coverage/, .session-feedback/, or .repository-hygiene/ as untracked after the rule is added.
No previously tracked files are hidden by the new patterns.
verification: Run git status --short and confirm the directories no longer appear as untracked.
Run git check-ignore -v coverage/ .session-feedback/ .repository-hygiene/ and confirm each path is matched.
non_goals: Do not delete the directories; only exclude them from tracking.

findings.title: Add repository-specific contribution guidelines
findings.summary: No recognized CONTRIBUTING.md exists in GitHub-supported repository locations.

evidence.observation: Missing CONTRIBUTING.md in .github/, repository root, and docs/

recommended_actions: Create CONTRIBUTING.md using repository-specific contacts, workflows, and commands.
Cross-link it from the README where appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: CONTRIBUTING.md exists in a GitHub-recognized location and contains no placeholders.
verification: test -f CONTRIBUTING.md || test -f .github/CONTRIBUTING.md || test -f docs/CONTRIBUTING.md
Review all commands and contacts against current repository evidence.

findings.title: Define maintainership boundaries with CODEOWNERS
findings.summary: The repository has multiple contributors but no CODEOWNERS file in a supported location.
recommended_actions: Map high-risk and domain-specific paths to active maintainers or teams.
Add fallback ownership and validate patterns against the current tree.
acceptance_criteria: Every critical path has an active, permission-valid owner and patterns match intended files.
verification: Review CODEOWNERS rendering on GitHub.
Open a test pull request touching representative paths.

findings.title: Review local branches whose upstream is gone
findings.summary: Local branches reference remote-tracking branches that no longer exist.
recommended_actions: Check each branch for unique commits and active worktrees.
Back up or push any work that must be retained.
Delete only branches proven obsolete.
acceptance_criteria: Every gone-upstream branch is retained with rationale, republished, or safely deleted.
verification: git branch -vv
git log --cherry --oneline <upstream>...<branch>

findings.title: Add a repository-specific pull request template
findings.summary: No GitHub-recognized pull request template was found.
recommended_actions: Add a concise template covering intent, risk, tests, documentation, and linked issue.
acceptance_criteria: New pull requests receive a useful, repository-specific template.
verification: Open a draft pull request and confirm the template is prefilled.

findings.title: Add structured issue intake templates
findings.summary: Issues are available or remote status is unknown, but no issue template or form was found.
recommended_actions: Create separate bug, feature, and support/security routing templates only where applicable.
Use required fields sparingly and ensure referenced labels exist.
acceptance_criteria: Issue intake captures reproducible evidence and routes non-issue requests appropriately.
verification: Validate issue-form YAML.
Open each template in GitHub's new-issue chooser.
